2605.03934.title: Towards Open World Sound Event Detection
2605.03934.paper_summary: Sound Event Detection (SED) plays a vital role in audio understanding, with applications in surveillance, smart cities, healthcare, and multimedia indexing. However, conventional SED systems operate under a closed-world assumption, limiting their effectiveness in real-world environments where novel acoustic events frequently emerge. Inspired by the success of open-world learning in computer vision, we introduce the Open-World Sound Event Detection (OW-SED) paradigm, where models must detect known events, identify unseen ones, and incrementally learn from them. To tackle the unique challenges of OW-SED, such as overlapping and ambiguous events, we propose a 1D Deformable architecture that leverages deformable attention to adaptively focus on salient temporal regions. Furthermore, we design a novel Open-World Deformable Sound Event Detection Transformer (WOOT) framework incorporating feature disentanglement to separate class-specific and class-agnostic representations, together with a one-to-many matching strategy and a diversity loss to enhance representation diversity. Experimental results demonstrate that our method achieves marginally superior performance compared to existing leading techniques in closed-world settings and significantly improves over existing baselines in open-world scenarios.
2605.03934.paper_summary_zh: 聲音事件檢測（SED）在音頻理解中扮演著至關重要的角色，應用於監控、智慧城市、醫療保健和多媒體索引等領域。<lb/>然而，傳統的SED系統在封閉世界假設下運作，這限制了它們在現實環境中的有效性，因為新型聲學事件經常出現。<lb/>受到計算機視覺中開放世界學習成功的啟發，我們引入了開放世界聲音事件檢測（OW-SED）範式，在這種範式中，模型必須檢測已知事件、識別未見事件，並從中逐步學習。<lb/>為了解決OW-SED的獨特挑戰，例如重疊和模糊事件，我們提出了一種1D可變形架構，利用可變形注意力自適應地聚焦於顯著的時間區域。<lb/>此外，我們設計了一個新穎的開放世界可變形聲音事件檢測Transformer（WOOT）框架，結合特徵解耦以分離類別特定和類別無關的表示，並採用一對多匹配策略和多樣性損失來增強表示的多樣性。<lb/>實驗結果表明，我們的方法在封閉世界設置中相較於現有的領先技術達到了邊際上更優的性能，並在開放世界場景中顯著改善了現有基準。
2605.03934.author: P. H. Hai et.al.
2605.03934.authors: P. H. Hai, L. T. Minh, L. H. Son
